interventions.description: Mailed FIT kits, Cologuard, and diagnostic colonoscopies.

interventions.name: Stool tests

identificationModule.briefTitle: Neoadjuvant Letrozole and Palbociclib in Patients With Stage II-IIIB Breast Cancer, HR+, HER2 -

interventions.name: Pre treatment Recurrence Score:18-25 Letrozole + Palbociclib
interventions.description: Letrozole(2,5 mg/day during 28 days of each Cycle) + Palbociclib(125mg/day during 21 days of each cycle of 28 days) as neoadjuvant treatment during 6 cycles before surgery of Breast Cancer for patients with pre-treatment recurrence Score of 18-25
armGroupLabels: Pre treatment Recurrence Score:18-25 Letrozole + Palbociclib

interventions.name: Pre treatment Recurrence Score:26-100 Letrozole + Palbociclib
interventions.description: Letrozole(2,5 mg/day during 28 days of each Cycle) + Palbociclib(125mg/day during 21 days of each cycle of 28 days) as neoadjuvant treatment during 6 cycles before surgery of Breast Cancer for patients with pre-treatment recurrence Score of 26-100
armGroupLabels: Pre treatment Recurrence Score:26-100 Letrozole + Palbociclib

identificationModule.briefTitle: Adjuvant Treatment in Premenopausal Breast Cancer

leadSponsor.name: Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS

conditions: Breast Cancer Early Stage Breast Cancer (Stage 1-3)

identificationModule.briefTitle: Anti-Hormone Therapy (With Anastrazole and Fulvestrant) Before Surgery to Treat Postmenopausal Women With Breast Cancer.

interventions.name: Fulvestrant
interventions.description: Fulvestrant IM on day 14 and on day 28 followed by day 28 of all subsequent cycles thereafter. Treatment will be continued for a total of 4 cycles.

interventions.name: Anastrazole
interventions.description: Anastrazole, 1mg by mouth every day of all 28 day cycles and continued for a total of 4 cycles

identificationModule.briefTitle: Prospective Evaluation of Breast-Conserving Surgery Alone in Low-Risk Ductal Carcinoma in Situ (DCIS)
Genomics in Michigan to AdJust Outcomes in Prostate canceR (G-MAJOR) for Men With Newly Diagnosed Favorable Risk Prostate Cancer

leadSponsor.name: University of Michigan Rogel Cancer Center

interventions.name: Decipher
interventions.description: The Decipher test (GenomeDx Biosciences) is a genomic test that will be performed on prostate biopsy tissue. Results will be provided to the patient and physician prior to making a treatment decision.
armGroupLabels: Standard of care + pre-treatment genomics testing

interventions.name: Prolaris
interventions.description: The Prolaris test (Myriad Genetics, Inc.) is a genomic test that will be performed on prostate biopsy tissue. Results will be provided to the patient and physician prior to making a treatment decision.
armGroupLabels: Standard of care + pre-treatment genomics testing

interventions.name: Oncotype Dx Genomic Prostate Score (GPS)
interventions.description: The Oncotype Dx GPS test (Exact Sciences corp.) is a genomic test that will be performed on prostate biopsy tissue. Results will be provided to the patient and physician prior to making a treatment decision.
armGroupLabels: Standard of care + pre-treatment genomics testing

interventions.name: Standard of care (askMUSIC score)
interventions.description: AskMUSIC is a risk calculator based on standard clinical and pathologic variables (https://ask.musicurology.com/) and is generally included as part of routine clinical care.
armGroupLabels: Standard of care (no pre-treatment genomics testing)
Standard of care + pre-treatment genomics testing

identificationModule.briefTitle: Informative Tools to Optimize Neoadjuvant Therapy in ER Positive, HER2 Negative Breast Cancers

interventions.name: Intervention 1
interventions.description: Ki-67 and Oncotype DX® will be performed on the baseline core biopsy specimen prior to initiation of neoadjuvant systemic therapy.

interventions.name: Intervention 2
interventions.description: MRI will be performed prior to initiation of neoadjuvant systemic therapy and at the end of treatment.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Neoadj ph 2 AI Plus Everolimus in Postmenopausal Women w/ ER Pos/HER2 Neg, Low Risk Score

interventions.name: Everolimus
interventions.description:
Aromatase inhibitor plus everolimus by mouth daily for 26 weeks. All patients will begin treatment on Cycle 1 Day 1 with both the standard dose of one of the following 3 aromatase inhibitors ( physician's choice) plus everolimus 10 mg by mouth daily:

* Anastrozole 1 mg
* Letrozole 2.5 mg
* Exemestane 25 mg

identificationModule.briefTitle: A Study of Cologuard™ in an Average Risk Population Assessing a Three Year Test Interval

interventions.name: Cologuard
interventions.description: Prescription of Cologuard for at-home stool collection

identificationModule.briefTitle: NeoTAILOR: ABiomarker-directed Approach to Guide Neoadjuvant Therapy for Patients With Stage II/III ER-positive, HER2-negative Breast Cancer

interventions.name: VENTANA MIB-1 Ki67 assay
interventions.description: Ki67 scoring determination (standard of care) utilizing the Ki67 MIB-1 assay (clone 30-9) (VENTANA) will be performed at baseline, Week 4 (+/- 14 days - high-risk group only), and at time of surgery in accordance with the International Ki67 in Breast Cancer Working Group guidelines.

interventions.name: Oncotype DX® Recurrence Score
interventions.description: Oncotype DX® Recurrence Score (RS) testing - assessing expression of 21 genes including 16 cancer-related genes and 5 reference genes - will be performed as standard of care in a central laboratory (Exact Sciences) on RNA extracted from formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded core-biopsy samples.

interventions.name: PAM50-based Prosigna breast cancer gene signature assay
interventions.description: This PAM50-based Prosigna breast cancer gene signature assay for intrinsic molecular subtype determination will be performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core-biopsy samples.

interventions.name: Anastrozole
interventions.description: Standard of care

interventions.name: Combination anthracycline and/or taxane based treatment
interventions.description: Standard of care
